evolutionSummary:
In the recent project evolution, we successfully processed a total of 140 items, comprising 104 issues and 36 pull requests (PRs). Throughout this period, no new devices were discovered, and there were no new feature proposals (FPs) submitted. However, the team remained proactive in addressing existing challenges, resolving numerous issues that contributed to the overall stability and functionality of the project.

Despite the absence of new FPs, the focus on issue resolution has led to significant improvements in the project's performance. The team is encouraged to continue monitoring for potential new devices and to explore opportunities for feature enhancements in future iterations. 

Action items moving forward include maintaining the momentum in issue resolution, encouraging community engagement for new device discovery, and prioritizing the development of new features based on user feedback. The ongoing management of issues and PRs will be crucial for sustaining project growth and user satisfaction.
